title: Self-evaluation
statusLabel: Design accepted for V1 — build starting
journey: School self-evaluation and inspection readiness usually lives across shared documents, spreadsheets, evidence folders, and ad-hoc follow-up meetings — hard to keep current and harder to hand over. This feature gives a school one structured workspace to record judgements against a real inspection framework (starting with the UAE framework, with others planned), assign sections of the review to the right staff member, and turn findings directly into tracked follow-up work. The plan for this one went through five rounds of independent review before being accepted — it's the most thoroughly designed feature on this list, and building against that plan is just getting underway.

subfeatures.name: Framework & standards model
subfeatures.note: The underlying data model for inspection frameworks, domains, and rating scales.

subfeatures.name: Judgement recording
subfeatures.note: Capturing a rating, confidence level, and rationale for each criterion, with full history.

subfeatures.name: Section assignment
subfeatures.note: Delegating specific domains or criteria to the right staff member.

subfeatures.name: Permissions & access
subfeatures.note: Who can view, judge, or manage which parts of the self-evaluation.

subfeatures.name: UAE framework content
subfeatures.note: The official UAE School Inspection Framework, pre-loaded and ready to use.

subfeatures.name: IB PSP framework content
subfeatures.note: The IB Primary Years self-study framework, as a second supported standard.

subfeatures.name: Review workspace
subfeatures.note: The main screen: pick a framework and academic year, see where the review stands.

testing.fixtureNote: The shared fixture includes one inspection framework, an active review cycle plus one from a prior year, both a whole-school judgement and a subject-specific one, and a case where review work is delegated to a non-management staff member — covering the main shapes this feature needs to handle.
